retrieved_titles: No Limit Kids: Much Ado About Middle School
retrieved_doc_snippets: The Moody Brothers | The Moody Brothers are an Americana country music trio who gained prominence in 1985 when they were nominated for a Grammy Award for their instrumental performance of the classic fiddle tune "Cotton Eyed Joe". This trio of brothers, Carlton Moody, Dave Moody, and Trent Moody were nominated for a second Grammy in 1990 and won three International Country Music Awards in Europe.

Dave Moody (sportscaster) | David Wayne "Dave" Moody is an American motorsports commentator who works for the Motor Racing Network. He is the lead turn announcer for MRN and has worked for the radio network on and off since 1983. He moved to MRN full-time in 1997 and became the lead turn announcer in 2001. Since 2003, Moody has been the host of the nationally syndicated Sirius XM show "Sirius XM Speedway" on Sirius XM NASCAR Radio.

Praise Band: The Movie | Praise Band: The Movie is a 2008 faith-based film by Elevating Entertainment Motion Pictures. It starred George Hamilton IV, Adam Melton and James Dana Bryan. The screenplay was written by Joshua Moody and the film was produced and directed by Dave Moody.

No Limit Kids: Much Ado About Middle School | No Limit Kids: Much Ado About Middle School is a 2010 direct-to-video family comedy film. It starred Bill Cobbs, Lee Meriwether, Blake Michael, Celeste Kellogg and Ashton Harrell. The screenplay was written by Joshua Moody and the film was directed by Dave Moody for Elevating Entertainment Motion Pictures, who also provided the musical score for the film.

Dave Moody | David B. Moody is an artist, producer, songwriter and filmmaker from North Carolina. His instrumental proficiency has earned him two Grammy Award Nominations and three International CMA Awards as a member of The Moody Brothers. After becoming regular performers on the Grand Ole Opry and touring extensively throughout Europe in the 80s, the trio of Carolina brothers signed a contract with the Walt Disney Company in 1992 to open and perform their own nightly concerts at Disneyland Par ...[truncated]

Moody's Manual | Moody's Manual is a series of manuals published by the Moody's Corporation. It was first published in 1900 by John Moody, nine years before he founded Moody's. Initially called "Moody's Manual of Industrial and Miscellaneous Securities", it was later superseded by "Moody's Manual of Railroads and Corporation Securities", then by "Moody's Analyses of Investments".

Moody Memorial Library | Moody Memorial Library is one of the two central libraries at Baylor University located in Waco, Texas. Jesse H. Jones Library is connected to Moody by two hallways and serves as the second of the two central libraries at Baylor. Moody is the larger of the two central libraries.
target_state.summary_2: Dave Moody — he fills more roles (musician/composer, songwriter, producer, director/filmmaker, etc.) than Ransom Riggs (primarily writer and filmmaker).
target_state.answer: Dave Moody — he fills a wider variety of roles (musician/composer, songwriter, producer, director/filmmaker, etc.) compared with Ransom Riggs, who is primarily a writer and filmmaker.
